All foreign nationals not in the 40-country free ETA list must obtain paid ETA. Tourist ETA fee: USD 20 (South Asian/SAARC countries, applied/at mission) or USD 25 (SAARC, on arrival); USD 50 (all others, applied/at mission) or USD 60 (all others, on arrival). Business ETA: USD 30 (SAARC) or USD 55 (all others); no on-arrival business ETA. Transit visa is free for 48-hour stays. Children under 12 receive free tourist ETA up to 30 days from any nationality.

visa_policy.notes: Sri Lanka operates an ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization) system for all arrivals. As of May 25, 2026, nationals of 40 countries receive a free 30-day tourist ETA. Maldivian nationals receive a 90-day free ETA under a bilateral reciprocal agreement. Seychelles and Singapore nationals also receive free ETA (30 days) under bilateral arrangements. All other nationalities pay a fee for ETA. Children under 12 from any nationality receive free tourist ETA. Sri Lankan dual citizens and children born to Sri Lankan parents are visa-exempt. Diplomatic/Official passport holders from specified countries visiting for official purposes may be exempt from ETA under bilateral agreements.

cbi.notes: Sri Lanka does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) programme. The official immigration website lists residence visa options (Golden Paradise, Investor Visa, My Dream Home, Resident Guest Scheme) but none confer a direct path to citizenship through investment. Dual citizenship is available to Sri Lankan diaspora under separate provisions at immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=4, not to foreign investors.

programs.name: Golden Paradise Residence Visa
programs.notes: Minimum deposit of USD 200,000 in a Golden Paradise Foreign Currency Account (GPFCA) at a bank licensed by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka. Up to 50% of the minimum investment may be withdrawn after the first year from visa issuance. Visa validity: 10 years. Visa fee: USD 2,000 for 10 years. Permitted investments: condominium property, land and building leases, Sri Lanka Development Bonds, other government-approved investments. Includes spouse and dependents. No official pathway to permanent residence or citizenship stated.

programs.name: Investor Visa Category
programs.notes: Minimum investment: USD 100,000 for 5-year visa or USD 200,000 for 10-year visa (amounts from search snippets attributed to immigration.gov.lk; full details in official PDF). Investment sectors include information technology, tourism, and other approved sectors. No official pathway to permanent residence or citizenship stated.

programs.name: My Dream Home Visa Programme
programs.notes: Fixed deposit requirement: USD 15,000 or equivalent in approved foreign currency at an approved Sri Lankan bank. Monthly remittance requirement: USD 1,500 per principal applicant; USD 750 per spouse and each dependent child. Visa validity: 2 years, renewable. Fee: USD 200 per year per person (applicants and dependents over 16). Currently open for extensions only (existing visa holders); not open to new applicants. No employment permitted. No pathway to permanent residence or citizenship stated.

programs.name: Resident Guest Scheme (RGS) Visa Programme
programs.notes: Extension-only program for existing RGS visa holders; not open to new applicants. Investor category: minimum USD 250,000 in a Foreign Currency Account; dependent: USD 35,000 minimum. Professional category: monthly income of USD 2,000 (applicant) and USD 1,000 per dependent. Visa validity: 5 years from issuance date. Fee: USD 200 per person per year. No employment permitted. No pathway to permanent residence or citizenship stated.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa Category
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who earn income remotely from clients or employers outside Sri Lanka; must have international health insurance covering Sri Lanka; requires recommendation from Ministry of Digital Economy
programs.notes: Applicants must prove minimum monthly remittance (exact amount in official PDF: immigration.gov.lk/content/files/visa/digital_nomad/Digital%20Nomad%20Visa%20Category.pdf - PDF not machine-readable from web). Digital nomad holders cannot engage in local employment; all income must be earned remotely. Application submitted to Residence Visa Division of the Department of Immigration and Emigration.

visa_types.name: Tourist ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization) – Free
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism, sightseeing, visiting friends/relatives, medical treatment, participating in arts, music, dance and sport events.
visa_types.notes: Free for nationals of 40 designated countries effective 25 May 2026. Double-entry facility permitted within validity. Maldivian nationals receive 90 days instead of 30. Seychelles and Singapore nationals also eligible for free ETA under bilateral arrangements. Children under 12 from any nationality receive free tourist ETA. Apply via eta.gov.lk.

visa_types.name: Tourist ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization) – Paid
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism, sightseeing, visiting friends/relatives, medical treatment, participating in arts, music, dance and sport events.
visa_types.notes: For nationalities not in the 40-country free ETA list. Fee: USD 20 (SAARC, applied online/at mission) or USD 25 (SAARC, on arrival); USD 50 (all others, applied online/at mission) or USD 60 (all others, on arrival). Double-entry facility permitted. Extensions possible for up to 6 months total. Some nationalities (e.g. Cameroon, Nigeria, Afghanistan, Syria) require a Sri Lankan sponsor and additional documents such as police clearance.

visa_types.name: Business ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization)
visa_types.purpose: Attending business meetings, negotiations, seminars, and conferences in Sri Lanka for a limited duration.
visa_types.notes: Fee: USD 30 for SAARC nationalities; USD 55 for all other nationalities. No on-arrival option for business ETA. Single, double, or multiple entry available. Apply via eta.gov.lk.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows travelers to transit through Sri Lanka ports and airports while continuing to their final destination.
visa_types.notes: Valid for 48 hours only. Free of charge. Requires proof of onward air ticket or sea passage. Employment, self-employment, and business activities prohibited. Apply via eta.gov.lk.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Employment Category
visa_types.purpose: For foreign professionals and expatriates employed in Sri Lanka, including BOI project personnel, bank employees, private company employees, and Colombo Port City workers.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Mandatory health protection plan required. Subcategories: government project professionals, BOI personnel, bank employees, private sector employees, Colombo Port City Commission workers. Dependents may accompany the primary visa holder. Appointment at Department of Immigration and Emigration required.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Investor Category
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals investing capital in Sri Lanka or conducting business activities under approved investment schemes.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment USD 100,000 (5-year visa) or USD 200,000 (10-year visa) in approved sectors such as IT and tourism. Separate guideline document available from the Department. No pathway to permanent residence or citizenship officially stated.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Educational Category
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals studying at Sri Lankan universities or approved educational institutions, as well as volunteers and interns.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Non-university students must show bank encashment receipts of at least USD 1,500 per year per person to prove sufficient funds. Subcategories: university students, approved institution students, volunteers, interns.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Medical Category
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undergoing medical treatment in Sri Lanka, along with one accompanying medical assistant.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Requires bank encashment receipts to the value of USD 1,500 per year per person to demonstrate ability to cover medical expenses. One medical assistant is permitted to accompany the patient.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Family Members (Spouse & Dependents)
visa_types.purpose: For non-Sri Lankan spouses of Sri Lankan citizens and dependent children of Sri Lankan citizens residing in Sri Lanka.
visa_types.notes: First issuance: 1 year free. Subsequent extensions: 2-year increments until 5 years total, then 5-year extensions. Dependent children aged 16 and over: USD 100 per year until age 22. Dependent children below 16 are free.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Religious Category
visa_types.purpose: For members of the clergy and foreign nationals participating in religious activities with the recommendation of the relevant ministry.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Requires organization request letter, ministry recommendation, passport biodata page, and a health protection plan.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – NGO/INGO Personnel
visa_types.purpose: For staff of non-governmental and international organizations operating in Sri Lanka with endorsement from the relevant ministry.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Relevant ministry endorsement required. Dependents may be included.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Ex-Sri Lankans & Dependents
visa_types.purpose: For former Sri Lankan citizens and their dependents wishing to reside in Sri Lanka.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Security clearance reports generally not required for this category. Dependents are eligible.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Court Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have pending court cases in Sri Lanka and are required to appear before Sri Lankan courts.
visa_types.notes: Renewable; each extension is valid for 14 days from the date of the next court hearing.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Diplomatic & Official Category
visa_types.purpose: For diplomats, embassy staff, and government officials at accredited diplomatic missions in Sri Lanka and their dependents.
visa_types.notes: Duration as recommended by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Gratis (no fee). Health protection plan exemption applies to this category.

visa_types.name: My Dream Home Visa Programme
visa_types.purpose: Residence visa for senior foreign nationals (retirees and those with independent income) wishing to reside long-term in Sri Lanka.
visa_types.notes: Currently open for extensions only; not accepting new applicants. Requires USD 15,000 fixed deposit in a Sri Lankan bank plus monthly remittances of USD 1,500 (principal applicant) and USD 750 per spouse/dependent child. Visa valid for 2 years, renewable. Fee: USD 200 per year per person (including dependents over 16). No paid or unpaid employment permitted.

visa_types.name: Resident Guest Scheme (RGS) Visa Programme
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence visa for high-net-worth foreign investors and skilled professionals wishing to reside in Sri Lanka.
visa_types.notes: Currently open for extensions only; not accepting new applicants. Investor category: minimum USD 250,000 in a Foreign Currency Account (USD 35,000 per dependent). Professional category: monthly remittance of USD 2,000 (applicant) and USD 1,000 per dependent. Visa valid for 5 years from issuance. Fee: USD 200 per person per year. No employment permitted. No pathway to permanent residence or citizenship officially stated.

visa_types.name: Golden Paradise Residence Visa
visa_types.purpose: 10-year residence visa for foreign investors making a qualifying deposit in a Sri Lankan bank under the Golden Paradise Foreign Currency Account scheme.
visa_types.notes: Minimum deposit of USD 200,000 in a Golden Paradise Foreign Currency Account (GPFCA) at a Central Bank of Sri Lanka-licensed bank. Up to 50% of the minimum investment may be withdrawn after the first year. Permitted investments include condominium property, land and building leases, Sri Lanka Development Bonds, and other government-approved investments. Fee: USD 2,000 for 10 years. Includes spouse and dependents. No official pathway to permanent residence or citizenship stated.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa – Digital Nomad Category
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who earn income remotely from clients or employers outside Sri Lanka and wish to reside in Sri Lanka.
visa_types.notes: Annual visa, renewable. Requires recommendation from Ministry of Digital Economy. Must have international health insurance covering Sri Lanka. All income must be earned remotely from outside Sri Lanka; local employment prohibited. Exact minimum income/remittance requirement listed in official PDF (immigration.gov.lk/content/files/visa/digital_nomad/Digital%20Nomad%20Visa%20Category.pdf). Application submitted to Residence Visa Division, Department of Immigration and Emigration.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 re-validation against live official sources (immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=60, immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=64, eta.gov.lk/slvisa/visainfo/fees.jsp, eta.gov.lk/slvisa/visainfo/center.jsp, immigration.gov.lk/index_e.php). CONFIRMED: 40-country free ETA list unchanged since 25 May 2026. CONFIRMED: Maldives 90-day free ETA, Seychelles/Singapore 30-day free ETA under bilateral reciprocal arrangements (fees page). CONFIRMED: All 29 bilateral conditional_access entries (passport-type exemptions) verified against live id=64 — no changes needed. CONFIRMED: ETA fee tiers unchanged. No new countries added or removed. No changes to the record required this cycle.
2026-06-13 re-validation against live official sources (immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=64, immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=60, eta.gov.lk/slvisa/visainfo/fees.jsp).

CONFIRMED ETA fee tiers: SAARC USD 20 (applied/mission) / USD 25 (on arrival); others USD 50 (applied/mission) / USD 60 (on arrival). Business visa SAARC USD 30 / others USD 55; no on-arrival business ETA. Transit free.
CONFIRMED Golden Paradise min USD 200,000 / 10-year visa / USD 2,000 fee.
CONFIRMED My Dream Home min USD 15,000 deposit / USD 1,500 monthly; extension-only.
CONFIRMED RGS investor min USD 250,000; professional USD 2,000/month; extension-only.
NOTE: All corrections based on live scrape of immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=64 on 2026-06-13; some ambiguity remains for Benin, Georgia, Hong Kong, Myanmar entries as page rendering may differ.

2026-06-27 re-validation against live official sources (immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=60, immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=64, eta.gov.lk/slvisa/visainfo/center.jsp, eta.gov.lk/slvisa/visainfo/fees.jsp).

CONFIRMED 40-country free ETA list (effective 25 May 2026): Australia, Austria, Bahrain, Belarus, Belgium, Canada, China, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, India, Indonesia, Iran, Israel, Italy, Japan, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, Malaysia, Nepal, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Oman, Pakistan, Poland, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Korea, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Turkey, UAE, UK, USA - matches record exactly.

CONFIRMED Maldives: 90-day free ETA under bilateral reciprocal agreement; Seychelles and Singapore: free ETA (30 days) required since May 25 2026 - all three must apply via ETA system (no longer visa-free on arrival without prior authorization).
CONFIRMED ETA fees unchanged from prior validation.

CORRECTED Hong Kong conditional_access 2026-06-27: passport_types changed from ['diplomatic','official','service'] to ['ordinary'] - live official page at id=64 lists 'Citizens of Hong Kong SAR' without any passport-type qualifier, indicating ordinary SAR passport holders qualify (not limited to diplomatic/official/service). This is a downgrade correction away from a false restriction.
CONFIRMED Benin conditional_access: Diplomatic, Official, Service - 30 days - matches record.
CONFIRMED Vietnam conditional_access: Diplomatic, Official - 90 days - matches record.

CONFIRMED all other 28 bilateral agreement entries (Bangladesh, Belarus, Brazil, Cambodia, Chile, China, Cuba, Georgia, India, Indonesia, Iran, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Maldives, Myanmar, Oman, Pakistan, Philippines, Qatar, Romania, Russia, Seychelles, Singapore, Thailand, Ukraine, Bahrain, Qatar) verified against live id=64 - no further changes needed.
gaps: Digital Nomad Visa exact minimum income/remittance requirement: official PDF (immigration.gov.lk/content/files/visa/digital_nomad/Digital%20Nomad%20Visa%20Category.pdf) was binary-encoded and could not be parsed; amount not confirmed from HTML sources.

Investor Visa 5-year vs 10-year exact minimum investment amounts (USD 100,000 / USD 200,000) not verified directly from HTML official page - sourced from search snippet attributed to immigration.gov.lk; full terms in official PDF (immigration.gov.lk/content/files/visa/Guideline%20Investor%20Visa%20Category%20doc%202.pdf) not machine-readable.

Complete enumeration of all countries with diplomatic/official/service passport agreements may not be fully listed on immigration.gov.lk/pages_e.php?id=64 - the page layout may have additional bilateral agreements not returned in web scrape.
Naturalization/permanent residence pathway years not officially published on immigration.gov.lk English pages.
No freedom-of-movement or common-travel-area agreements found for Sri Lanka on official sources; SAARC does not have a free movement treaty.
Golden Paradise Visa PDF brochure (immigration.gov.lk/content/files/visa/golden_paradose_brochure.pdf) was binary-encoded and could not be parsed for full details.

Type A credential-based conditional entry (visa-free/VoA/ETA for holders of US/UK/Schengen/Australian visa or residence permits): NO such rule found on any official Sri Lankan immigration source. Sri Lanka does not appear to offer preferential entry based on holding a third-country visa or residence permit.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder special entry rule for Sri Lanka: NOT found on any official Sri Lanka immigration source.
Paid ETA on-arrival availability for Business Visa: official fees page states no on-arrival option for business ETA - updated in paid ETA notes field.

outbound_official.notes: no official Sri Lanka government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Exhaustive search of mfa.gov.lk, immigration.gov.lk, eta.gov.lk (2026-06-14): MFA and Department of Immigration only publish INBOUND visa policy (for foreign nationals entering Sri Lanka); no origin-side page listing countries Sri Lankan ordinary-passport holders may enter without a prior consular visa was found on any *.gov.lk domain.
